items.title: 'Everything looked real': How scammers are hijacking your holiday

children.content: Australian travellers are being left out of pocket and fearful their personal data has been stolen, amid growing concerns over the security of online travel bookings.

gallery.altText: Close up of the booking.com website.

thumbnail.altText: Close up of the booking.com website.

items.title: Shots fired where gangster's funeral was advertised to be held

children.content: The ABC understands the funeral of Sydney gangster Lorenzo Lemalu was originally scheduled to take place at the venue in Punchbowl, but the plans were changed.

image.altText: a window with a hole after it was shot

items.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany, WA

children.content: A male diver has died after an attack by a suspected 4.5-metre shark near Michaelmas Island, off WA's Albany coast, on Saturday morning.

image.altText: police albany 3

items.title: "We were so close": Laos cave rescue called off as conditions deteriorate

children.content: The search for two men trapped in a flooded cave in Laos has been postponed as rescuers say a "miracle" is needed.

image.altText: Rescuers stand under torchlight near the entrance of a cave in Laos

items.title: A Sydney mother died after drinking 67 cups of water under 24-hour watch

children.content: When Tammy Shipley felt herself sliding into psychosis, she made a heartbreaking decision: She walked into a police station and asked to be arrested, believing prison would keep her safe. Here's what happened next.

image.altText: A mother holds a boy in her arms. They both smile. The boy wears an orange and red jumper.

items.title: How Everest guide survived six days alone with no oxygen and a few chocolates

children.content: A few chocolates found in his pocket and an avalanche may have been what saved Dawa Sherpa from almost certain death on Mount Everest.

image.altText: Dawa Sherpa being rescued.

gallery.altText: A Nepali man in a blue snow suit

thumbnail.altText: A Nepali man in a blue snow suit

items.title: Power hang on for thrilling win over Eagles, Dockers claim record win

children.content: Port Adelaide hangs on for a thrilling win over West Coast after Logan Morris kicked seven in a Brisbane QClash win and Fremantle beat North Melbourne by 124 points to claim the biggest win in its history.

image.altText: Port Adelaide players hug and and congratulate each other after a win

items.title: Broncos' season in crisis as Titans young gun conjures golden moment

children.content: Keano Kini scores one of the great game-winning tries to condemn Brisbane to a fifth-straight defeat, with the Titans snatching a 28-23 victory at Lang Park.

image.altText: A rugby league player in black has his hands on his head and is looking glum.

items.title: What's driving Australia's lentil boom?

children.content: Australia's winter crop production is expected to be down this year, but lentils are bucking the trend and headed towards a record.

items.title: Mexico score late winner against sloppy Matildas

children.content: The lacklustre Matildas have been beaten by Mexico for the first time in over 20 years, losing 1-0 in Newcastle.

image.altText: Diana Ordonez runs with her arms out

items.title: Volpato admits he was 'a bit too scared' to make switch from Italy

children.content: New Socceroo Cristian Volpato opens up on his decision to switch allegiance from Italy to Australia ahead of the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato brushes his hair with left hand in training

items.title: Inflatable frogs and chicken suits: How protests are approaching Trump 2.0

children.content: Demonstrators are increasingly employing inflatable costumes and whimsy. But behind the dress up, these protesters argue it is "serious play".

items.title: Australia, NZ back friendship to endure 'volatile world'

children.content: Global volatility and economic turbulence have dominated annual talks between the leaders of Australia and New Zealand, Anthony Albanese and Christopher Luxon.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese and Christopher Luxon standing next to one another in dark suits with their hands clasped.

items.title: Russian oil facilities hit by second Ukrainian drone attack after Putin rejects talks

children.content: One person is dead and an oil depot has been set ablaze after a series of drone attacks waged by Ukraine on the Russian city of St Petersburg.

image.altText: A plume of black smoke is seen over the port of St. Petersburg, Russia,

items.title: A dreamy, creamy chicken soup you'll want to eat out of the pot

children.content: Looking for an easy warming meal? This creamy chicken soup has everything you need for a weeknight dinner.

items.title: Mother creates modest activewear to empower herself and others

children.content: Yasmin Jammoul was sick of wearing oversized men's T-shirts to the gym when she started exercising postpartum, so she created her own clothing line.

image.altText: A woman with a dark green hijab and black outfit sitting on a chair smiling.

items.title: Wall Street suffers worst hit of 2026 so far amid massive stock sell-off

children.content: The US stock market has experienced its worst losses in months due to large sell-offs of shares in technology giants and fears of a looming rate hike in the wake of a strong jobs figures report.

image.altText: A male stockbroker seen from behind looking at a white Wall Street shares graph on a computer screen.

items.title: Fastest-rising women's cancer in Australia still has a low profile

children.content: The incidence of uterine cancer has doubled over the past 25 years. But it's also a disease without a public profile, which means many women aren't aware of the warning signs or the risk factors.

gallery.altText: A composite image of a baby in a trolley next to a portrait image of a woman smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of a baby in a trolley next to a portrait image of a woman smiling.

items.title: Woman charged with hindering police investigation after man dies at Tweed Heads

children.content: Police have arrested a man and charged a woman after a man died following an alleged assault on the New South Wales North Coast.

image.altText: A suburban street with police tape and a police van in the foreground

items.title: Lorna has lived through two world wars, two pandemics and 26 Australian prime ministers

children.content: After a couple of years as the country's oldest woman, Lorna Henstridge is still going strong.

image.altText: An elderly woman holding an oval framed photo of a young child wearing Elizabethan costume

items.title: Nightmare turnover leaves Wembanyama, Spurs in NBA Finals hole

children.content: Victor Wembanyama turns the ball over and misses two shots in a horror final minute as the Knicks take a 2-0 series lead back to New York.

items.title: Hawks forward target of racist online abuse after loss to Bulldogs

children.content: The AFL and Hawthorn come out in support of Mabior Chol after the Hawks forward shares a series of abusive messages he received after Friday night's match against the Bulldogs.

image.altText: Mabior Chol takes a mark for the Hawks during an AFL match.

items.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds

children.content: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

gallery.altText: Andrew seen through the window of a car, with a red, purplish mark on his cheek.
A close up of Prince Andrew looking over his shoulder and wearing a suit.

thumbnail.altText: Andrew seen through the window of a car, with a red, purplish mark on his cheek.
A close up of Prince Andrew looking over his shoulder and wearing a suit.

items.title: New AUKUS risk as Trump orders 'Golden Fleet' battleships

children.content: A committee of Congress wants the US Navy to explain how building "Trump-class" battleships will not delay other nuclear-powered boats, such as submarines needed for AUKUS.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands next to a posterboard showing a "Trump-class" submarine

items.title: The US ally getting nuclear-powered submarines with no AUKUS deal

children.content: South Korea is taking a different path to Australia in building nuclear-powered submarines, but its plan still hinges on the United States.

image.altText: Three men in white uniforms and caps salute at the entrance on top of a large grey submarine, with a South Korean flag behind.

items.title: Patients 'chained' at Sri Lankan nursing home where 13 died in fire

children.content: A "chained" patient was among more than a dozen people who died during a fire at an unregistered nursing home in Sri Lanka, according to a staff member.

image.altText: A man looks out through bars inside a nursing home

items.title: US and Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The US military says it has shot down four drones Iran has launched toward the Strait of Hormuz and struck some of Tehran's coastal surveillance radar sites in response.

image.altText: A group of people paddle in water.

items.title: Korda stages comeback as four Aussies advance at US Open

children.content: World No.1 Nelly Korda edges closer to the top of the leaderboard at Riviera as Grace Kim leads the way for the Australian contingent.
